texto: I guess I could be pretty pissed off about what happened to me, but it’s hard to stay mad when there’s so much beauty in the world. Sometimes I feel like I’m seeing it all at once, and it’s too much; my heart fills up like a balloon that’s about to burst. And then I remember to relax, and stop trying to hold onto it. And then it flows through me like rain, and I can’t feel anything but gratitude—for every single moment of my stupid, little life. You have no idea what I’m talking about, I’m sure; but don’t worry….you will someday.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Alan Ball: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “I guess I could be pretty pissed off about what happened to me, but it’s hard to stay mad when th…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, alan-ball, american-beauty pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e american-beauty.

Sometimes I feel like I’m seeing it all at once, and it’s too much; my heart fills up like a balloon that’s about to burst. And then I remember to relax, and stop trying to hold onto it. And then it flows through me like rain, and I can’t feel anything but gratitude—for every single moment of my stupid, little life. You have no idea what I’m talking about, I’m sure; but don’t worry….you will someday.

texto: The idea of celibate vampires is ridiculous. To me, vampires are sex. I don't get a vampire story about abstinence. I don't care about high school students. I find them irritating and uninformed.
